channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: 'We share same ambition' - Alonso named Chelsea's third boss in year
item.description: Former Liverpool midfielder Xabi Alonso is appointed as Chelsea manager - a day after their FA Cup final defeat by Manchester City.

item.title: No trophy in turbulent season but Alonso arrival offers Chelsea hope
item.description: From the gloom of an FA Cup final loss amid a turbulent campaign, Xabi Alonso's arrival gives Chelsea hope, writes Nizaar Kinsella.

item.title: Casemiro's fond farewell - how Brazilian turned Man Utd career around
item.description: Casemiro plays his last game for Manchester United at Old Trafford against Nottingham Forest, and will return a hero no matter what the future holds after turning his career around at the club.

item.title: The Alonso dream is over - but Salah's outburst keeps heat on Slot
item.description: Xabi Alonso may now be heading to Chelsea, but Mohamed Salah's outburst keeps the pressure on Arne Slot, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: From non-league to FA Cup hero - Semenyo caps rise with 'incredible' goal
item.description: The FA Cup tends to produce fairytale stories and Antoine Semenyo's sensational winning goal in this season's final provided the latest in chapter in the storied history of the world's oldest cup competition.

item.title: Resilient Celtic time run perfectly to win race after eight-month chase
item.description: Celtic chased Hearts for eight months, 32 games, 2,880 on-field minutes and 48 hours. In the season's dying embers, they caught them.

item.title: Tipped for relegation to title winners - AGF's 'King Arthur' moment
item.description: Aarhus GF were tipped for relegation from the Danish Superliga this season. Instead, they will lift the championship trophy for the first time in 40 years this Sunday.

item.title: Ronaldo's trophy wait continues as Al-Nassr upset in final
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo's wait for a first major trophy with Al-Nassr continues after they are upset 1-0 at home by Gamba Osaka in the AFC Champions League Two final.

item.title: 'Wow!' - Semenyo scores 'special' goal to win FA Cup for Man City
item.description: Antoine Semenyo scores a "special" goal as Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 win the FA Cup final.

item.title: Balotelli, a Brazil winger and the bid for Serie D promotion
item.description: Chievo Verona are having to rise from the bottom once more, but have had some unlikely help from Mario Balotelli and ex-Brazil winger Douglas Costa.

item.title: 'They always deliver' - Man City savour 'magical' title
item.description: For the first time in seven seasons, the ribbons on the Women's Super League trophy are a different shade of blue after Manchester City's "magical" triumph.

item.title: Celtic lift Premiership trophy after incredible ending
item.description: Watch Celtic lift the Scottish Premiership trophy for the fifth season in a row, denying Hearts on an astonishing final day.

item.title: Man City beat Chelsea after brilliant Semenyo goal
item.description: Antoine Semenyo's brilliantly-improvised backheel is enough to give Manchester City a 1-0 win over Chelsea in the FA Cup final, as Pep Guardiola's side complete a domestic cup double at Wembley.

item.title: Shaw stars as champions Man City complete big win over West Ham
item.description: Khadija Shaw scores twice as WSL champions Manchester City end their campaign with an impressive 4-1 away win against West Ham United.

item.title: Kane nets hat-trick as Bayern thrash Cologne
item.description: England captain Harry Kane scores his fourth hat-trick of the season as Bundesliga champions Bayern Munich thrashed Cologne in their last league game of the campaign.

item.title: Manchester City lift WSL trophy after win at West Ham
item.description: Champions Manchester City ended their Women's Super League campaign on a high, beating West Ham with Khadija Shaw scoring twice, before lifting the trophy.

item.title: Arsenal clinch second spot in WSL with win at Liverpool
item.description: Russo scores twice as Arsenal beat Liverpool to finish second in the WSL clinching an automatic spot in the Women's Champions League.

item.title: 'Menacing and threatening atmosphere' forced Hearts exit
item.description: Hearts say "the menacing and threatening atmosphere inside the stadium" forced them to leave Celtic Park immediately after the final whistle of their Scottish Premiership decider.

item.title: 'Celtic gave me reasons to live' - but O'Neill unsure about staying on
item.description: Martin O'Neill says Celtic have given him "reasons to live" after their stunning Scottish Premiership triumph, but the 74-year-old insists he does not know whether he will stay on beyond this season.

item.title: Why summers of change beckon for WSL giants
item.description: With players departing and improved seasons needed, there could be summers of change for Arsenal and Chelsea.

item.title: Mourinho completes unbeaten season with Benfica
item.description: Jose Mourinho completes an unbeaten league season with Benfica but has to settle for third place in the Portuguese top-flight.

item.title: Liverpool must return to heavy metal football - Salah
item.description: Mohamed Salah says Liverpool must return to being a "heavy metal attacking team that opponents fear" after trophyless season.

item.title: Juventus to step up Silva efforts - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Juventus are hoping to beat their rivals to sign Manchester City's Bernardo Silva, Barcelona continue their interest in Chelsea's Joao Pedro, Coventry City look at a move for Dutch midfielder Georginio Wijnaldum, plus more.

item.title: Why Hearts' title tilt is unlikely to be a one-off
item.description: Hearts may have missed out on a first Scottish title for 66 years, but Tony Bloom and his data analytics company are out to ensure they are challenging for the title every season.

item.title: Truth stranger than fiction as O'Neill leads Celtic from hostility to happiness
item.description: Martin O'Neill said Celtic would have to win seven out of seven in the run-in to win the Scottish Premiership - here is how they did it.

item.title: Man Utd reach agreement to appoint Carrick manager
item.description: Manchester United reach an agreement with Michael Carrick to become the club's permanent head coach.

item.title: The engineer missing his shift at Wembley... to play there instead
item.description: Engineer Jay Lovell was set to be working at Wembley on Sunday, but instead he will be playing there in the FA Vase final.

item.title: What Man City's FA Cup win means for Premier League places in Europe
item.description: The top eight in the Premier League will now qualify for Europe - and sixth could yet end up in the Champions League. Here's how.

item.title: Lewandowski to leave Barcelona as 'mission is complete'
item.description: Poland striker Robert Lewandowski will leave Barcelona at the end of the season when his contract expires.

item.title: 'It's happened in training' - Semenyo on FA Cup winner
item.description: Antoine Semenyo expresses his gratitude for being able to "shine" and "compete for all the top trophies," after his winning goal secured the FA Cup for Manchester City at Wembley.

item.title: Kerr equals record in final game as Chelsea beat Man Utd
item.description: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea's joint all-time top goalscorer alongside Fran Kirby, as her 116th club goal on her final appearance gives Sonia Bompastor's side a 1-0 win over Manchester United at Stamford Bridge.

item.title: Wolfsburg beat St Pauli to earn relegation play-off reprieve
item.description: Wolfsburg earn a 3-1 away win over St Pauli to relegate the home side to Bundesliga 2, while the visitors must now win a relegation play-off to avoid dropping down to the German second division.

item.title: Guardiola pays tribute to departing Silva and Stones
item.description: Pep Guardiola pays tribute to departing players Bernardo Silva and John Stones following his side's 1-0 FA Cup final win over Chelsea at Wembley.

item.title: Stones on 'nice send off' after lifting FA Cup trophy
item.description: Manchester City's John Stones admits it has been a "difficult" season for him but is pleased to get a "nice send off" after lifting the FA Cup trophy following their 1-0 win over Chelsea at Wembley.

item.title: London City beat Aston Villa to finish sixth
item.description: Freya Godfrey scores an injury-time winner as London City Lionesses come from behind to beat Aston Villa and finish their debut Women's Super League season in sixth place.

item.title: Aston Villa secure Champions League spot with win over Liverpool
item.description: Ollie Watkins scores twice as Aston Villa move above Liverpool into fourth in the Premier League and seal qualification for next season's Champions League.

item.title: Arbeloa would be happy if Mourinho returns to Real
item.description: Real Madrid head coach Alvaro Arbeloa says he would be pleased if Jose Mourinho replaces him and leads the club next season.

item.title: Three Lions - the football anthem that united a nation
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to Three Lions co-creator David Baddiel about the song that united a nation.

item.title: South Korea captain Son picked for fourth World Cup
item.description: Former Tottenham Hotspur striker Son Heung-min will captain South Korea at his fourth World Cup this summer.

item.title: Chopping trees and country music - how Paterson will prepare for Wembley
item.description: Martin Paterson will be chopping trees and listing to country music as he begins preparations for Notts County's League Two play-off final at Wembley.

item.title: 'We crumbled' - another loss, another unwanted record for leaky Liverpool
item.description: Arne Slot says his Liverpool side "crumbled" during their thumping defeat by Aston Villa, as the pressure on him continues to mount.

item.title: Why Newcastle's history makers are breaking up
item.description: Newcastle United ended a 70-year wait for a major domestic trophy by winning the League Cup last season but further members of that side look set to leave.

item.title: The making of the £60K bargain who became an Everton legend
item.description: As Seamus Coleman announces he will leave Everton this summer, BBC Sport looks at the rise of the £60,000 signing who became a club legend.

item.title: What next for Chelsea & Kerr as striker exits?
item.description: Sam Kerr's arrival at Chelsea in 2020 was the biggest transfer in Women's Super League history - but what next for the club as Australia striker exits?

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Blossoms & Songer
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on indie band Blossoms and rapper Songer plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for the FA Cup final and this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: What does Premiership climax mean for European places?
item.description: What does Saturday's dramatic and chaotic climax to the Scottish Premiership top-six mean for European football next season?

item.title: Rangers to pursue Moore return - gossip
item.description: Rangers want to bring on-loan winger back for next season as Falkirk prepare to sell striker if price is right.

item.title: Man charged after referee Beaton and family put under police surveillance
item.description: The SFA say referee John Beaton and his family were put "under police surveillance" in the wake of a recent penalty controversy, with police confirming a man had been arrested in connection with a data leak.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
